title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona evaluation benchmarks fail to detect "Miranda distortion," where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, causing persona systems to replicate cultural composites rather than specified characters. He proposes a four-stage paradigm shift to epistemic simulation with domain expert evaluation loops and introduces the pre-registered Prism Experiment using historical figures and anachronism detection rubrics.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas argues that current persona evaluation benchmarks miss "Miranda distortion," a failure mode where cultural representations in training data overwhelm primary documentary records, so persona systems reproduce cultural composites instead of the specified character. The stakes: standard persona evals can pass systems that are actually replicating popular portrayals rather than the historical figure. The talk proposes a four-stage shift toward epistemic simulation with domain expert evaluation loops, and introduces the pre-registered Prism Experiment, which uses historical figures and anachronism detection rubrics to test temporal fidelity and character consistency.
